content.text: The accusative case is used for the direct object — the noun directly affected by the action of the verb. For example: 'I loved the book,' 'I watched the movie,' 'I liked the meal.' In these sentences, book, movie, and meal are all in the accusative case.
Here is an example of first declension nouns in the accusative case:
The –ην ending is the first declension accusative singular ending.
